news.title: 'Less than human': how unhoused people in Atlanta are being treated during the World Cup – video
news.lede: “This is what happens when you apply these incredibly violent economic forces to these cities … it's happened at every World Cup I've been to,” explains our chief sports writer, Barney Ronay. Employees in Atlanta, Georgia, recently threw away tents, medication, identification and other belongings of unhoused people at a public park without warning. This led activists and a local official to point to an apparent violation of procedures created after a city employee ran over a tent with a front loader last year, killing a man. Cornelius Taylor was crushed inside his home as workers came to clear a homeless encampment.

news.title: World Cup schedule today pits England vs. France for third place in Miami
news.lede: England and France meet in the 2026 World Cup third-place playoff on July 18 with pride, momentum and a podium finish on the line. Both teams looking to finish the tournament on a high note after falling short in the semifinals. England will rely on its mix of veteran leaders and young stars, while France hopes its talented lineup can close out the World Cup with a win. Here's everything you need to know about today's match, including kickoff time, TV information and betting odds.

news.title: Enzo Fernández shows real quality for Argentina but Chelsea future is up in the air
news.lede: Brash midfielder has admirers in Madrid, though his World Cup displays illustrate what he can offer Xabi Alonso’s Blues. Enzo Fernández was an unstoppable mix of style and spite during Argentina’s win over England. The midfielder’s first contribution during Wednesday’s semi-final in Atlanta was to plough into Elliot Anderson and clobber Manchester City’s new £116m signing round the head. He escaped without a booking, though, and made the most of some generous refereeing by showing the other side to his game when Argentina took control after going 1-0 down early in the second half.

news.title: World Cup Golden Boot 2026: Who will take the trophy?
news.lede: The race for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Golden Boot has been one for the ages this summer. It’s been a summer where the big names have shone, and the Golden Boot is firmly up for grabs with just two games to go. France face England in the third/fourth play-off this evening, with several stars from both sides still in the mix for the prize. Kylian Mbappe is the joint-leading scorer at the World Cup heading into the final fixtures, and is aiming to become the first player ever to win multiple Golden Boots at the tournament.

news.title: Tuchel defiant in face of criticism over decisions in England exit: ‘I have no regrets’
news.lede: Head coach rejects ‘blame game’ after Argentina exit. President Trump joins critics of Thomas Tuchel’s tactics. Thomas Tuchel has insisted he is not interested in engaging in the “blame game” over England’s World Cup elimination and vowed to close the gap on the leading nations. Even Donald Trump has joined criticism of Tuchel’s tactics in the semi-final defeat by Argentina, with the US president questioning why he made Harry Kane “a defensive player” as England attempted to hold on to a 1-0 lead.

news.title: Merino: Arsenal star reveals common ground with Lautaro ahead of World Cup Final
news.lede: Mikel Merino admits he and Lautaro Martinez find themselves in a similar situation ahead of the World Cup Final: ‘For no player is starting on the bench the ideal game plan.’ Merino’s Spain and Lautaro Martinez’s Argentina meet in the World Cup Final tomorrow, Sunday, July 19. The Spanish midfielder spoke to Gazzetta dello Sport ahead of the game, admitting that he and Lautaro might not start. “For no player is starting on the bench the ideal game plan,” he said.

news.title: The World Cup Third-Place Play-Off: Is It the Weirdest Game in Football?
news.lede: France will play England in the 2026 World Cup third-place play-off on Saturday. We look back on what some see as a bit of an odd fixture at the tournament. After 102 games, there are just two remaining at the 2026 World Cup. One is the biggest game in football, the World Cup final.

news.title: 6.5 million fans attend World Cup 2026: White House
news.lede: Spectator attendance at the 2026 FIFA World Cup has surpassed 6.5 million, more than the combined total of the previous two tournaments, the executive director of the White House Task Force on the FIFA World Cup 2026 said. Andrew Giuliani made the comments during a joint news conference with Mora Namdar, deputy assistant secretary of state for consular affairs, in New York ahead of Sunday's World Cup final, where the pair addressed security measures in place in and around the city. "We are wrapping up the highest-attendance, most-watched, safest and most successful World Cup in history," Giuliani said, adding that more than 6.5 million people have watched matches, a figure he said tops the combined attendance of the previous two tournaments.
